paper_title: Material and methods from a study on low order streams in central Brazil

methods.description: Process for collecting periphyton samples from various substrates in low order streams during different seasons.

steps.description: Periphyton was collected during the dry (August) and rainy (March) seasons, between the years 2012 and 2013, according to the protocols established by the ComCerrado Network.
steps.searchable_summary: collect periphyton samples according to ComCerrado Network protocols

steps.description: In each stream it was delimited a 50 m longitudinal transect, where the following substrates, wherever possible, were considered: leaf litter (L), macrophytes (M), roots (R), trunk (T), pebble (P) and sand (S).
steps.searchable_summary: delimit 50 m transect and collect samples from specified substrates

steps.description: It was used a syringe to collect sand; macrophytes were squeezed and the other substrates were scraped with a toothbrush, using distilled water gentle jets.
steps.searchable_summary: collect substrate samples using syringe, squeezing, and scraping methods

steps.description: Samples were preserved in 3-5% formalin solution and deposited in the Herbarium of the University of Goiás (UFG).
steps.searchable_summary: preserve samples in formalin and deposit in herbarium

methods.description: Measurement of abiotic variables in the field using standard electrodes.

steps.description: Along with periphyton, some abiotic variables (water temperature, dissolved oxygen, pH and electrical conductivity) were measured in the field using standard electrodes (Yellow Spring Instruments).
steps.searchable_summary: measure abiotic variables using standard electrodes

methods.description: Cleaning and taxonomical analysis of periphyton samples using oxidation method and light microscopy.

steps.description: Periphyton samples were cleaned using the oxidation method of Simonsen (1974) modified by Moreira-Filho & Valente-Moreira (1981).
steps.searchable_summary: clean periphyton samples using modified oxidation method

steps.description: The samples were fixed on permanent mounts with Naphrax® resin.
steps.searchable_summary: fix samples on permanent mounts with Naphrax resin

steps.description: Taxonomical analyzes were performed using a light microscope Leica DM500 equipped with digital camera ICC50Hd.
steps.searchable_summary: perform taxonomical analysis using light microscope

steps.description: The identification was based on valve morphological and morphometric features, using both classic and regional literature.
steps.searchable_summary: identify species based on morphological features using literature
